labels.notes: Labelled-as-Catherine-Deveuge 2:35pm WAS ACTUALLY SHIRLEY RICE — vision mislabel (chart-stuck-on-wrong-patient). Two earlier Catherine D-something sessions (1:22 mention + 1:59 actual) had primed the EMR chart; clinician didn't switch the chart for Shirley's visit, so vision OCR captured the prior Catherine Deveuge for 12 of 15 minutes (18:19–18:31 UTC) before the v0.10.45 re-sample throttle finally fired at 18:51 — past the encounter's 18:50 end. Vision early-stop locked in at K=5 with the wrong name; DOB invalidation never fired (vision returned no DOB on any call). DEFECTS: (1) Wrong patient identified — generalized solution: cross-check vision name against transcript greeting patterns ('Hi Shirley'); (2) PROCEDURE SECTION HALLUCINATED — 3 entries: 'Reviewed blood work and ECG', 'Provided printed copy', 'Completed DTC form'. None are billable procedures. Same v0.10.61 overcapture pattern; (3) DIAGNOSTIC CODE 249 (Pre-diabetes) — HbA1c 6.7% is borderline pre-diabetes range, but the visit's primary content is bilateral knee OA with significant functional impairment + DTC application. Expected dx 715 (Osteoarthritis); (4) Billing A007A + K030A + Q040A acceptable for knee OA + counselling-style DTC discussion — looks reasonable, though could justify A004A given the multi-issue (knees + DTC + DM + nutrition) discussion.
